title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Jaundice
intro_desc: If you're dealing with jaundice, you might want to try using herbal essential oils that have been traditionally used to help with this condition. Jaundice is a yellowing of the skin and eyes caused by a buildup of bilirubin in the blood, which can be due to liver problems or other issues.  One herb that has been used for centuries to help with jaundice is Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle. It's believed to help protect the liver and promote its natural cleaning processes, which can help to reduce the levels of bilirubin in the blood.  Ginger, or Zingiber officinale, is another herb that has been used to help with jaundice. It's thought to help stimulate digestion and improve liver function, which can also help to reduce bilirubin levels.  Turmeric, or Curcuma longa, is another herb that has been traditionally used to help with jaundice. It's believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and to help improve liver function, which can help to reduce the symptoms of jaundice.  Herbal teas made from these herbs can be a gentle and natural way to help with jaundice. They can be made by steeping the herbs in hot water, and then straining and drinking the liquid.  Using herbal teas to help with jaundice can bring several benefits to your life. For one, it can help to reduce the symptoms of jaundice, such as the yellowing of the skin and eyes. It can also help to improve your overall sense of well-being, by promoting the natural cleaning processes of the liver and improving digestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains a high concentration of silymarin, a complex mixture of flavonoids and polyphenols. Silymarin has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which help to protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals. The sesquiterpene lactones, particularly eudesmanolides and germacranolides, in Silybum marianum essential oils have been found to have hepatoprotective properties, which can help to reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function. The essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce liver damage caused by jaundice, a condition characterized by an accumulation of bilirubin in the blood. By reducing liver inflammation and protecting liver cells, Silybum marianum essential oils may help to alleviate the symptoms of jaundice.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum seeds, 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil), and a clean glass jar.
Combine Silybum marianum seeds and carrier oil in the glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Seal the jar and leave it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the seeds.
Store the Silybum marianum essential oil in a dark glass bottle, away from heat and light. Use 2-3 drops per day to help with jaundice symptoms under medical guidance.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains zingiberene and gingerols, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, a common cause of jaundice. Gingerols also have a direct effect on the liver, stimulating the production of bile and improving liver function. The anti-inflammatory properties of zingiberene and gingerols may also help to reduce the severity of jaundice by reducing liver cell damage. Regular use of Zingiber officinale essential oils may help to alleviate jaundice symptoms by supporting liver health and function.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh ginger roots and 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Bring the water to a boil.
Reduce heat and add the ginger roots. Simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a bowl using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve. Discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) to the liquid. Stir well.
Let the mixture sit for 2-3 days to allow the essential oils to separate. Strain and store the oil in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a compound called turmerone, which is made up of various bioactive constituents, including curcumenol, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are thought to be beneficial in reducing liver inflammation associated with jaundice. The antioxidants in Curcuma longa essential oils, particularly curcumenol, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells and contribute to the development of jaundice. Additionally, the bioactive constituents in Curcuma longa essential oils have been shown to improve liver function and increase the flow of bile, which can help to alleviate jaundice symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties of Curcuma longa essential oils, particularly bisdemethoxycurcumin, may also help to reduce liver inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Curcuma longa roots and clean them with water. Let them dry for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a blender or grinder.
Mix 1 cup of the powder with 4 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture in a saucepan over low heat (around 150°F) for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally.
Filter the mixture using a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container. Let it cool and use the essential oil as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function. The essential oil's calming and relaxing properties can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to liver problems. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties can help protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation, improving liver function, and protecting the liver from damage, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers, 1 cup of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil), and a clean glass jar.
Add 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the glass jar, then pour in 1 cup of carrier oil. Stir well.
Cover the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day to help the oil extract from the flowers.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid. Use 5-7 drops of this oil in a carrier oil for skin application to help soothe jaundice symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains boiactive constituents like camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce liver inflammation, which is often associated with jaundice. The essential oil's antioxidant properties can also help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, Rosmarinus officinalis has been shown to have choleretic properties, which help stimulate bile production and improve liver function. This can help alleviate jaundice symptoms by promoting the excretion of bilirubin from the body.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat and add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves. Let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a still or a distillation apparatus and heat it until the water evaporates, leaving the essential oil behind.
Collect the Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a clean glass bottle and label it. Use 5-7 drops in a carrier oil for topical application for jaundice.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains limonene, menthone, and menthol as its main bioactive constituents. These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, a key factor in jaundice. The menthol in Mentha x piperita essential oils also has a stimulating effect on the liver, promoting its function and helping to remove bilirubin from the body. Additionally, the limonene in the oil has a cleansing effect on the liver and gallbladder, further supporting the removal of bilirubin. By reducing inflammation and promoting liver function, Mentha x piperita essential oils may help alleviate the symptoms of jaundice.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves, 2 cups of water, a pot, a strainer, and a glass jar for storage.
Combine the fresh Mentha x piperita leaves and water in the pot. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through the strainer into a glass bowl, squeezing the leaves to get the most oil out. Discard the leaves.
Let the mixture sit for 2 hours to allow the oil to separate from the water. Then, use a spoon to scoop the oil off the top and transfer it to the glass jar.
Store the Mentha x piperita essential oil in the glass jar in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the oil per day to help relieve jaundice symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium essential oils contains a compound called thujone, which has been shown to have a positive effect on jaundice. Thujone has a hepatoprotective property, meaning it helps protect the liver from damage. This is due to the presence of other bioactive constituents, such as camphor and borneol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells, while the anti-inflammatory properties reduce liver inflammation and promote healing. By reducing liver inflammation and protecting the liver from damage, Artemisia absinthium essential oils can help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Artemisia absinthium herbal essential oils for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Artemisia absinthium leaves, stems, and flowers.
Place the gathered plant material in a clean glass jar and cover it with 2 cups of carrier oil (like olive or coconut oil).
Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the plant material and store the extracted essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils contains limonene, linalool, and beta-pinene as its bioactive constituents. These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and infection associated with jaundice. The essential oil's antispasmodic properties, attributed to the presence of linalool, help to relax the muscles and reduce bile duct spasms that can contribute to jaundice. Melissa officinalis essential oils' antioxidant properties, driven by limonene, help to protect the liver from oxidative damage and promote its recovery. By reducing inflammation and promoting liver health, Melissa officinalis essential oils may help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 500ml of Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a clean bowl.
Steam distill the Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a distillation apparatus for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities.
Store the Melissa officinalis essential oil in a clean, dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium essential oils contains sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and volatile compounds as its bioactive constituents. The sesquiterpenes, specifically chamazulene, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation associated with jaundice. The flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that protect liver cells from damage and promote healthy liver function. The volatile compounds, including linalool and beta-pinene, have antispasmodic properties that help alleviate liver spasms and improve liver function. By reducing inflammation, protecting liver cells, and alleviating liver spasms, Achillea millefolium essential oils can help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal essential oils for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium flowers, a glass jar with a lid, and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Fill the glass jar 2/3 with dried flowers and add 2 cups of carrier oil. Stir well and seal the jar.
Place the jar in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every 2 days. This is the infusion time.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container. Discard the solids.
Bottle the essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the oil per day for jaundice relief under medical supervision.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains limonene, geraniol, and citral as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with jaundice. Limonene, in particular, has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, protecting the liver cells from damage and promoting liver function. The antioxidant properties of citral and geraniol help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to liver damage and promote the body's natural detoxification processes. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Cymbopogon citratus essential oils may help alleviate jaundice symptoms and support liver health.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the Cymbopogon citratus leaves to the saucepan and let them simmer for 30 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 10 minutes. Strain the liquid into a bowl using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Use a dropper to transfer the essential oil into a dark glass bottle. Store it in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains compounds like limonene, beta-pinene, and eucalyptol. These constituents have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and inflammation in the body, which are common symptoms associated with jaundice. Eucalyptol also has a liver-protecting effect, which can aid in the removal of bilirubin from the body and help alleviate jaundice symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties of limonene and beta-pinene can help soothe the digestive system and reduce discomfort. By reducing fever and inflammation, and promoting liver health, eucalyptus globulus essential oils may help alleviate the symptoms of jaundice.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water in the saucepan and add the Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 2-3 hours to release the oil from the leaves.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container.
Let the oil separate from the water and collect it in a small bottle for use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce the risk of jaundice in newborns. Some herbal teas, like dandelion and milk thistle, have been shown to support liver health. These teas may aid the liver in removing bilirubin, the pigment that causes jaundice. However, more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for jaundice are not a substitute for medical treatment. While some herbs like milk thistle may help, consuming them daily is not a safe solution. The liver needs specific medical care to recover from jaundice. Herbal teas might interact with medications or worsen the condition if not used properly.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with jaundice, but it's hard to say exactly how long it takes to see results. Some people notice improvements in a few days, while others may take a week or more to feel better. It really depends on the severity of the jaundice and the individual's response to the herbal teas.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for jaundice is best in the morning. This helps your body process the tea's active ingredients throughout the day, potentially reducing jaundice symptoms. Morning also gives you a chance to start the day feeling refreshed and more alert.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas may interact with medications for jaundice. For example, St. John's Wort can make certain medications less effective. Licorice root can also increase the risk of liver damage when taken with certain medications. These interactions can be serious, so it's essential to be aware of the potential risks when combining herbal teas with prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: It's not recommended to drink herbal teas for jaundice while pregnant. Some herbal teas can be too strong and might affect the baby's health. Jaundice is a condition that needs medical attention, and herbal teas might not be enough to treat it. It's best to wait for a medical check-up and follow the doctor's advice.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for jaundice while breastfeeding aren't recommended. Some herbs can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. For example, dandelion root tea can lower breast milk production and sage tea can slow down digestion in the baby. It's best to choose teas that are safe for breastfeeding mothers and their babies.
title_2: Baby Jaundice and Herbal Essential Oils: 11 Remedies to Help
